subject: Biology
title: Meiosis: Shuffling the Deck
description: A short lesson to help you apply Meiosis: Shuffling the Deck.

hook.text: Meiosis makes sex cells with half the chromosomes—23 instead of 46 in humans.

buildup.text: Two rounds of division turn one cell into four genetically unique cells.

discovery.text: Crossing over swaps segments between chromosomes, creating new combinations.

twist.text: Without meiosis, every child would be a clone. Genetic variation would vanish.

climax.text: When sperm meets egg, two half-sets unite into a full genome—a new individual.

punchline.text: Meiosis makes everyone one of a kind.

quiz.question: How many cells does meiosis produce from one starting cell?
